enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about credit and debit cards. He criticizes current high interest rates (as high as 23%) as historically extortionary compared to Roman-era caps, blaming deregulation and corporate influence on politics. He predicts government will eventually take over banking within 50-100 years, interest rates will drop to around 2%, and microchip implants will replace physical payment methods.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: extortionary and historically unprecedented; deregulation and corporate political influence are to blame
government will take over banking within 50-100 years, which he sees as a fair outcome
will drop to around 2% once government controls banking
people should be able to opt in and eventually most will adopt them as life becomes easier without wallets
society is trending toward socialism/communism, citing Obamacare as an early step

claims.subject: credit and debit cards
claims.claim: Credit and debit cards are currently being used with high interest rates, such as 23%, 19%, and 12%.

claims.subject: credit card interest rates
claims.claim: In the past, interest rates were much lower, such as 4% or even less in Roman times.

claims.subject: credit card interest rates
claims.claim: The current high interest rates are partly due to a lack of regulations and businesses pushing the envelope.

claims.subject: credit card interest rates
claims.claim: High interest rates are also a result of businesses needing to make up for people who don't pay their debts.

claims.subject: credit card regulations
claims.claim: The lack of regulation in the credit card industry has led to interest rates going out of control.

claims.subject: politics and business
claims.claim: Big businesses control politics, which affects regulations in industries like credit cards.

claims.subject: future of credit cards
claims.claim: In 50 to 100 years, credit cards will still exist but will become more regulated and socialistic.

claims.subject: government and banking
claims.claim: The government will eventually take over the banking system as industries become more regulated.

claims.subject: future interest rates
claims.claim: Within 100 years, interest rates on credit cards and loans will likely decrease to around 2%.

claims.subject: future payment methods
claims.claim: In the future, people may opt for microchip implants for payments, making wallets unnecessary.

claims.subject: microchip implants
claims.claim: Microchip implants for payments will become more common as people see the convenience and become less fearful of government control.

claims.subject: future society
claims.claim: In 50 to 100 years, society may evolve to the point where people walk around with everything stored in tiny chips in their bodies.
